mission.title: metallb: Remove the config reloading fail-static behavior
mission.description: Remove the config reloading fail-static behavior. Requested by 10+ users.

steps.title: Check current metallb deployment
steps.description:
Verify your metallb version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metallb
helm list -n metallb-system 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working metallb installation.

steps.title: Review metallb configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant metallb configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metallb
kubectl get configmap -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=metallb
```
Right now, MetalLB tries hard to fail static if you delete its configuration, or provide an invalid/unsafe configuration.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Remove the config reloading fail-static behavior
steps.description:
regarding 1, on my testing deleting the MetalLB controller pod seems to work. The pod gets re-created and then MetalLB re-runs the reconciliation logic using the new config.
Until we remove the config reloading safety mechanism, MetalLB "remembers" the old IP and refuses to update it to a new IP to
```yaml
data:
  config: |
    address-pools:
    - name: default
      protocol: layer2
      addresses:
      - 192.0.2.110-192.0.2.125
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metallb-system -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metallb
kubectl get events -n metallb-system --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Remove the config reloading fail-static behavior" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
regarding 1, on my testing deleting the MetalLB controller pod seems to work. The pod gets re-created and then MetalLB re-runs the reconciliation logic using the new config.
Until we remove the config reloading safety mechanism, MetalLB "remembers" the old IP and refuses to update it to a new IP to avoid breaking things.
codeSnippets:
data:
  config: |
    address-pools:
    - name: default
      protocol: layer2
      addresses:
      - 192.0.2.110-192.0.2.125

{"caller":"main.go:114","configmap":"metallb-system/config","error":"new config not compatible with assigned IPs: service \"decco/k8sniff\" cannot own \"192.168.28.4\" under new config","msg":"applying new configuration failed","op":"setConfig","ts":"2019-08-09T21:40:20.150041105Z"}
{"caller":"k8s.go:338","configmap":"metallb-system/config","error":null,"event":"configStale","msg":"config (re)load failed, config marked stale","ts":"2019-08-09T21:40:20.150070566Z"}

- name: default
      protocol: layer2
      avoid-buggy-ips: true
      addresses:
      - 192.0.2.10/32
      - 192.0.2.11/32 
      - 192.0.2.12/32
      auto-assign: true

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with metallb installed or the issue environment reproducible.
